Applying `sudo()` to the full `request.env` (not just the model recordset) widens privileges for every subsequent ORM call in the same scope; always narrow it to a single model as shown.

def _portal_ensure_token(self):
    """ Get the current record access token """
    if not self.access_token:
        # we use a `write` to force the cache clearing otherwise
        # `return self.access_token` will return False
        self.sudo().write({'access_token': str(uuid.uuid4())})
    return self.access_token
gotchas: `self.sudo().write(...)` is required (not just field assignment) because Odoo's ORM field cache is NOT invalidated by a plain `self.access_token = ...` assignment on a stored field — the stale cached value would be returned.
`portal.mixin` adds `access_token` but does NOT add `access_url` logic for your model — you must override `_compute_access_url` to return a meaningful URL otherwise `access_url` is always `'#'`.

Never expose `access_token` in a context key or URL fragment that is logged by a proxy/firewall; treat it as a secret credential — even a Read-only portal share token grants full record read access to anyone who has it.

snippet_text:
countries = request.env['res.country'].sudo().search([])
states = request.env['res.country.state'].sudo().search([])

values = {
    'partner': partner,
    'countries': countries,
    'states': states,
    'has_check_vat': hasattr(request.env['res.partner'], 'check_vat'),
}
gotchas: Portal users typically have no read rights on `res.country` by default — `sudo()` is needed for multi-country selector widgets; omitting it raises `AccessError` silently handled as a 500 in production.

`.sudo().search([])` fetches ALL records regardless of `ir.rule` domain restrictions — if the model has multi-company rules, always add an explicit company filter: `.sudo().search([('company_id', '=', ...)])` or `('company_id', 'in', request.env.companies.ids)`.

`request.env['res.partner']` used bare (without sudo) still reflects the portal user's rights — `hasattr` for optional module features like `check_vat` is safe because it is a Python attribute check, not an ORM query.

`default_get` receives only the list of field names declared in the view — if a field is not in the view it will NOT be in the `fields` arg; always guard with `if 'my_field' in fields` before setting it to avoid silently ignored defaults.

Always call `super().default_get(fields)` first and update the returned dict — skipping super loses defaults set by parent classes (e.g. `_defaults` class attribute or `default=` on field definition).

`self.env.context.get('active_ids')` can be a list of IDs from multi-select; `active_id` is only the last-clicked record in a list view and may differ from `active_ids[0]` when records are selected in reverse order — always prefer `active_ids` for batch wizards.

Returning `{'type': 'ir.actions.act_window_close'}` closes the dialog and reloads the underlying list/form; returning `None` or omitting a return also closes the dialog but does NOT reload — use `act_window_close` when changes must be reflected immediately.

To re-open the current wizard (multi-step): return a new `ir.actions.act_window` dict pointing to the same `res_model`; the previous wizard record stays in the DB until the `ir.autovacuum` cron cleans stale `TransientModel` rows.

Returning an `ir.actions.act_window` dict that opens another model's form view (e.g. to show the created invoice) requires setting both `res_model` AND `res_id` for a single-record form view; omitting `res_id` opens a new empty form.
`default_get` must return One2many/Many2many values as ORM command tuples `(0, 0, vals)` for create — NOT as plain dicts; the webclient deserialises the return value differently from `create()`.

`patch()` modifies the prototype in-place globally — patching a component used in multiple views will affect ALL instances; use `owl Component inheritance` (extend the class) instead when you only need to override behaviour in one context.

gotchas: `tracking=True` on a field (v12+ syntax) replaces `track_visibility='onchange'` — in v11 use `track_visibility='onchange'` or `'always'`; the `tracking` kwarg is silently ignored in v11
`mail.activity.mixin` adds the Activities tab to the form — it requires `mail.thread` to also be in `_inherit`; adding `mail.activity.mixin` alone causes a MRO error

`portal.mixin` provides `access_url` and `_compute_access_warning` — include it only on top-level business documents (sale.order, purchase.order), not on line models, to avoid portal performance overhead
The order in `_inherit` list matters for MRO — put `mail.thread` before `mail.activity.mixin` to avoid method resolution conflicts in the chatter setup hooks

class FleetVehicleLogContract(models.Model):
    _name = 'fleet.vehicle.log.contract'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']
    _inherits = {'fleet.vehicle.cost': 'cost_id'}
    _description = 'Contract information on a vehicle'
    _order = 'state desc, expiration_date'

    cost_id = fields.Many2one(
        'fleet.vehicle.cost',
        string='Cost',
        required=True,
        ondelete='cascade',
    )
gotchas: `_inherits` delegates field reads AND writes to the parent model via the FK (`cost_id`) — the parent record is auto-created on `create()` if not supplied; do NOT supply both `cost_id` and parent fields in the same `vals` dict or you get a duplicate record

The delegation FK field (`cost_id`) MUST be `required=True` and `ondelete='cascade'` — missing `required=True` allows orphan child records; missing `cascade` leaves dangling parent rows after child delete
`_inherits` vs `_inherit`: `_inherits` creates a SEPARATE table with a FK (composition); `_inherit` extends the SAME table (extension) — confusing them causes the wrong table structure

Fields on the parent model appear on the child as if declared locally — you can override them in `_columns`/class body, but the ORM may still write to the parent table; always test with `ir.model.fields` inspection after override
`@api.model` methods receive `self` as the model class (no records) — never iterate `self` or call `self.ensure_one()` inside an `@api.model` method
Using `@api.model` as a `default=` value: pass the method reference without calling it (`default=_default_journal`, NOT `default=_default_journal()`)

In v13+ `@api.model` is still valid for class-level factory methods, but `@api.model_create_multi` must be used for `create()` overrides — using `@api.model` on `create()` in v13+ causes a deprecation warning and breaks batch import
`self._context` inside `@api.model` is a frozen dict — never mutate it; use `self.with_context(key=val)` to create a new environment with an extended context
`inverse=` method is called when the user writes to the computed field directly — without it the field is read-only in both UI and ORM; the UI will show it as non-editable even with `readonly=False`

The `inverse` method receives `self` as a recordset and must update the underlying source fields — do NOT call `self.write({'priority': ...})` inside `_set_priority` as this creates infinite recursion
`store=True` on a computed field creates a DB column and triggers recompute via triggers — without `store=True`, the `inverse` method still works but the value is never persisted to the DB
`@api.one` decorator (v12 pattern) was removed in v13 — rewrite `_compute_priority` to loop `for picking in self:` explicitly if migrating to v13+
In v12 `create()` already accepts `vals_list` (list of dicts) — the `@api.model_create_multi` decorator backport from v13 is NOT needed in v12; use the bare `def create(self, vals_list):` signature
Always `return super().create(vals_list)` and use the returned recordset — modifying `vals_list` items in-place before `super()` is correct; never call `super()` inside the loop per-item
Accessing `line.order_id.state` AFTER `super().create()` triggers a separate SQL query per line — batch the post-create logic with `lines.filtered(...)` and `mapped()` to minimise N+1 queries

f-strings require Python 3.6+ — v12 runs Python 3.6; v11 runs Python 3.5 where f-strings are also available; but v9-v10 are Python 2/3 hybrid so f-strings are NOT safe in addons targeting those versions
`_check_company_auto = True` on the model auto-validates Many2one fields that have `check_company=True` kwarg — without it you must write the constraint manually for every relational field
`@api.constrains` fires AFTER write/create, not before — you cannot cancel the operation inside; raise `ValidationError` to rollback the transaction
`@api.constrains` is NOT called by `_sql_constraints` — both run independently; use `_sql_constraints` for DB-level uniqueness and `@api.constrains` for Python-level cross-field logic

`force_company` context key (v13) sets the company context for journal/sequence lookups — it was replaced by `allowed_company_ids` in v14; using `force_company` in v14+ code silently falls back to the user's primary company

`force_company` in v13 switches the active company for `ir.sequence` numbering, journal selection, and `company_id` defaults — it does NOT switch the security context (access rights still use `allowed_company_ids`)
In v14+, `force_company` is deprecated — use `self.with_company(company_id)` instead; `with_company()` updates both `force_company` and `allowed_company_ids` atomically

`self = self.with_context(...)` rebinds the local variable but does NOT mutate the caller's `self` — any code in the caller that uses its own `self` after calling this method still operates under the original context

When `with_context(force_company=X)` is used inside a `create()` override, the child records created by `super()` do NOT automatically inherit the context — you must pass the context explicitly to nested `env[model].create()`
`@api.constrains` must list ALL fields whose change should trigger the check — missing a field means the constraint is skipped on that write even if the field's value invalidates the constraint
Raising `ValidationError` inside `@api.constrains` triggers a full transaction rollback — any changes made before the constraint method ran are also rolled back

`@api.constrains` is NOT triggered by `_sql_constraints` violations — `_sql_constraints` raises `IntegrityError` (psycopg2); both are caught by Odoo and shown as user-friendly errors but are independent
Do NOT call `self.write()` or `self.create()` inside `@api.constrains` — it risks infinite recursion if the written field is in the constraint trigger list

from odoo import api, fields, models

class SaleOrder(models.Model):
    _name = 'sale.order'
    _inherit = [
        'portal.mixin',
        'mail.thread',
        'mail.activity.mixin',
        'utm.mixin',
    ]
    _description = 'Sales Order'
    _check_company_auto = True

    state = fields.Selection(
        [('draft', 'Quotation'), ('sale', 'Sales Order')],
        default='draft',
        tracking=True,
    )
gotchas: `tracking=True` field kwarg was introduced in v13 and is the v14+ standard — `track_visibility='onchange'` still works in v14 but emits deprecation warnings; remove it in new code
`utm.mixin` adds campaign/source/medium tracking fields — include it on top-level marketing documents only; it adds 3 Many2one fields and an index per record which can impact bulk create performance

`_check_company_auto = True` requires all Many2one fields that need company scoping to declare `check_company=True` — without it the auto-check only covers fields with the domain `[('company_id', '=', company_id)]`
In v14, the `@api.multi` decorator is removed — any addon importing it from `odoo.api` will get an `ImportError`; remove all `@api.multi` decorators when upgrading to v14

`odoo.define('module.Name', function(require) {...})` is the v14 legacy module system — in v15+ use `/** @odoo-module **/` with ES6 `import`; the legacy system still works in v15 via a compatibility shim but is deprecated
`this._super.apply(this, arguments)` is the legacy prototype chain method (Owl-less) — in OWL components use `super.setup()` and `super.methodName()` instead
`Widget.extend({template: 'XmlId'})` requires the QWeb template to be loaded via `<template>` in the manifest — if the template XML id doesn't exist, the widget silently renders nothing
SystrayMenu registration (`SystrayMenu.Items.push(...)`) is v14 legacy pattern — in v15+ use the systray registry: `registry.category('systray').add('name', Component, {sequence: 1})`

v14 uses OWL 1 (`owl.hooks.onMounted`, `owl.Component`) wrapped inside the legacy `odoo.define` module system — in v15+ use `/** @odoo-module **/` with `import { onMounted } from "@web/core/utils/hooks"`
`owl.hooks` (v14) was split into individual imports in v15 — `const { onMounted } = owl.hooks` becomes `import { onMounted } from '@odoo/owl'` in v16+; v15 is the bridge using `const { ... } = owl;`
The `useListener` helper from `web.custom_hooks` (v14) is removed in v15 — replace with `useExternalListener(target, event, handler)` from `@web/core/utils/hooks`
OWL 1 (v14) components cannot use `useService()` directly — they must access services via `this.env.services.service_name`; the `useService` hook from `@web/core/utils/hooks` is a v15+ API

`/** @odoo-module **/` (with double asterisks) is required — `/* @odoo-module */` (single asterisk) is silently ignored and the file stays in the legacy module system, causing import errors at runtime

In v15, `Component` and `useState` are accessed via `const { Component, useState } = owl;` — in v16+ they are imported directly: `import { Component, useState } from "@odoo/owl"`; mixing the two styles causes undefined errors
`QWeb.registerComponent('Name', MyComponent)` is v15 OWL 1 registration — in v16+ components are registered via the component registry or used directly in templates without registration
`Dropdown.props` must be declared as a static property for prop validation — omitting it in v15 silently accepts any props; in strict mode (v16+) missing prop declarations cause runtime warnings

`usePosition(reference, { popper: 'refName' })` — the `popper` option is a `useRef` NAME string, not an element; the hook looks up `useRef(popper).el` internally; passing an HTMLElement directly silently breaks
The `reference` argument can be an HTMLElement OR a function returning one — use a function when the reference element is not yet in the DOM at hook setup time (e.g. inside an `onMounted`)
`usePosition` is a v15-specific internal hook — it was refactored in v16 to use CSS `position: fixed` + `useFloating`; copying v15 hook code verbatim into a v16 addon causes positioning glitches
The position bus (`POSITION_BUS`) is shared within the component subtree via `useSubEnv` — multiple nested popovers reuse the same bus; triggering 'update' on parent also repositions all children

`patch(Class.prototype, 'unique.identifier', {...})` in v15 takes a THREE-argument form with a string identifier — in v16+ the identifier argument was REMOVED: `patch(Class.prototype, {...})`; using the v15 form in v16 raises TypeError

Always call `this._super()` (v15 patch) or `super.method()` (v16 OWL patch) inside patched methods — skipping super breaks the original method chain and can cause the component to malfunction silently
In v15, `patch()` is from `@web/core/utils/patch`; the `Class.include({...})` Odoo legacy method was removed in v16 — ensure the import path is correct before patching

Patching a getter (like `get columns()`) via `patch()` works in v15 but requires the patch object to define it as a getter too — defining it as a regular method makes it return the function instead of calling it

<record id="view_sale_order_custom_filter" model="ir.ui.view">
    <field name="name">sale.order.search.custom</field>
    <field name="model">sale.order</field>
    <field name="inherit_id" ref="sale.view_sales_order_filter"/>
    <field name="arch" type="xml">
        <xpath expr="//filter[@name='my_sale_orders_filter']"
               position="after">
            <filter name="custom_filter"
                    string="Custom"
                    domain="[('state', '=', 'draft')]"/>
        </xpath>
    </field>
</record>
gotchas: Search view filters use `domain=` attribute as a Python string (evaluated server-side) — do NOT use JSON syntax or Odoo boolean literals; use Python list syntax: `"[('field', '=', value)]"

The `expr` in `<xpath>` for search views targets filter/separator/group elements by their `name` attribute — targeting by position index (`//filter[2]`) breaks when other modules insert filters before yours
In v10, `<filter>` nodes inside a search view `<xpath position="after">` must be complete filter declarations — you cannot inject partial attributes; the entire `<filter>` element must be provided

`position="after"` inserts the new node as the NEXT sibling — if the target filter is the last in its group, the new filter appears correctly; but if there's a `<separator/>` after the target, your filter appears between the target and the separator

Multiple stored fields sharing one `compute=` method must ALL be declared with `store=True` and `compute='_method'` — if only one is `store=True`, the others are recomputed on every read (no caching), causing N+1 queries

`track_visibility='onchange'` (v10 syntax) was renamed to `tracking=True` in v13 — keep using the old form only if the codebase targets v10/v11/v12 specifically; mixed usage causes migration tool confusion

`@api.depends('order_line.price_total')` — the dotted path traverses the o2m relation; if `price_total` is itself a computed field, it must also have `store=True` or the dependency chain breaks and the parent field is never recomputed

When `store=True` fields are recomputed, Odoo runs a SQL UPDATE for each record in the batch — for large recordsets (>1000 records) this can cause table locks; use `--limit-time-cpu` and batch manually if running manual recomputes
`t-field` uses the ORM field's widget for rendering (date formatting, currency symbol, etc.) — `t-esc` or `t-out` outputs the raw Python repr; never use `t-esc` for Monetary fields in reports
QWeb report templates registered as `ir.actions.report` must have `report_type='qweb-pdf'` for PDF output — omitting it defaults to HTML preview and the report engine skips wkhtmltopdf
In v11, `docs` is the standard variable name for the record batch passed to the report template — renaming it requires updating the `binding_model_id` action AND the template variable reference
`t-foreach` without `t-as` is a syntax error in strict QWeb — always provide both attributes; the `t-as` variable is available as both `var_name` (value) and `var_name_index` (0-based index)

gotchas: `model_id:id` uses the auto-generated external ID `model_<model_name_with_dots_as_underscores>` — for `sale.order` the ID is `model_sale_order`; wrong model ID causes a silent access record that references nothing
Leaving `group_id:id` empty grants access to ALL users including public portal — always specify a group unless you explicitly want world-readable/writable access
`perm_unlink=1` for a salesman group is almost always wrong — salespeople should not delete confirmed orders; use `0` and create a dedicated manager rule

CSV header must be exactly `id,name,model_id:id,group_id:id,perm_read,perm_write,perm_create,perm_unlink` in that order — Odoo parses by column index, not header name; wrong column order silently assigns wrong permissions

from odoo import api, fields, models

class SaleOrderLine(models.Model):
    _name = 'sale.order.line'
    _check_company_auto = True

    order_id = fields.Many2one(
        'sale.order',
        string='Order Reference',
        required=True,
        ondelete='cascade',
        index=True,
    )
    product_id = fields.Many2one(
        'product.product',
        string='Product',
        domain="[('sale_ok', '=', True)]",
        check_company=True,
    )
gotchas: `domain=` on a Many2one field filters the dropdown selection — it does NOT enforce a database-level constraint; users can bypass it via direct RPC; add `@api.constrains` if enforcement is required

`check_company=True` (v13+) auto-validates the Many2one against the record's `company_id` when `_check_company_auto = True` is set on the parent model — without it, cross-company product links are silently allowed

`ondelete='cascade'` on `order_id` means deleting a `sale.order` also deletes all its lines — if you override `unlink()` on the parent to prevent deletion, lines are still cascade-deleted before your check runs
`index=True` on a Many2one FK is almost always correct — without it, filtering or grouping by `order_id` does a full table scan; Odoo does NOT add FK indexes automatically unlike most ORMs

<record id="view_order_tree" model="ir.ui.view">
    <field name="name">sale.order.tree</field>
    <field name="model">sale.order</field>
    <field name="arch" type="xml">
        <tree string="Quotations">
            <field name="name"/>
            <field name="partner_id"/>
            <field name="amount_total"
                   optional="show"/>
            <field name="date_order"
                   optional="hide"/>
        </tree>
    </field>
</record>
gotchas: `optional="show"` makes the column visible by default but user-hideable; `optional="hide"` makes it hidden by default but user-showable — columns without `optional` are always visible and cannot be hidden by the user

The optional column visibility state is saved per user/view in `ir.ui.view.custom` — in multi-user environments changes by one user do not affect others; system administrators cannot force a global default via UI

`optional` attribute was introduced in v14 — using it in views targeting v12/v13 causes a server-side QWeb rendering error; guard with `<field name="optional">show</field>` approach for mixed-version addons

When adding `optional` to an existing column via `<xpath position="attributes">`, the attribute must be added BEFORE the view is cached — a server restart or module upgrade is needed to see the change
Always declare `static template` pointing to an existing QWeb t-name — missing template causes a silent render crash without a useful error message in the console

`static supportedTypes` restricts which ORM field types can bind to your widget — omitting it means ANY field type can bind, which is usually not intended and can cause runtime errors when the field value shape doesn't match your component expectations

`extractProps` receives `{ attrs, field, record }` — attrs are the view XML attributes (e.g. options), field is the ORM field descriptor; always return an object even if empty or Owl will throw a props validation error
In Odoo v17, the field registry category is `'fields'` (not `'field_components'` from v14-v15) — check `registry.category('fields')` call, not `fieldRegistry` import which was removed
Register the widget in `web.assets_backend` (not `web.assets_frontend`) for backend views; frontend (portal/website) widgets go in `web.assets_frontend`

snippet_text: // JS side — setup() and event handler setup() { // no useService / onWillStart needed for minimal widget } onInput(ev) { this.props.update(ev.target.value); }
gotchas: `props.update(newValue)` is the canonical way to commit a field value change — do NOT mutate `props.value` directly or call `record.update()` from the widget; the field binding handles dirty state and re-rendering
For Many2one fields, `props.value` is `{ id, display_name }` (or `false`) — not a bare integer; always guard with `props.value && props.value.id`
For Selection fields, `props.value` is the selection key string (not the translated label) — use `props.options` to get `[key, label]` pairs for rendering a dropdown
Omit `static forceSave = true` unless you need to write the field even when unchanged (e.g. always-write fields) — setting it unnecessarily triggers extra RPC calls
Template must be included in the same `<templates>` bundle loaded in `web.assets_backend`; cross-bundle template references fail silently and render an empty widget
